strand: Word reading: letter sounds, digraphs and sound-blending (Reception)
gapNote: Phonics blending is pitched at KS1; no EYFS-level grapheme-to-sound or first-blend tool with single sounds and 10+ digraphs.
title: Sound Button Blender
concept: Press a sound button under each grapheme to hear it, then drag them together so the class blends the word aloud, starting with VC/CVC and the core digraphs.

strand: Grammar and punctuation: capitals, full stops, question/exclamation marks, conjunctions
gapNote: Sentence Builder includes capitals and full stops but not ?/!, coordination/subordination (and/but/because) or expanded noun phrases.
title: Punctuation Power-Up
concept: Read a sentence aloud with different end marks; the class drags the correct . ? ! and the sentence changes meaning/intonation to show statement vs question vs command.

strand: Word reading: morphology, prefixes, suffixes and word origins
gapNote: Etymology Explorer touches roots/origins but is pitched at KS3 and not focused on KS2 decoding of new words via prefixes/suffixes.
title: Prefix and Suffix Builder
concept: Drag super-, anti-, auto-, -ate, -ify onto root words; the app shows how meaning and pronunciation shift so pupils decode unfamiliar words.

strand: Spelling and punctuation: homophones, apostrophes, commas, colons/semicolons and dashes
gapNote: Punctuation for ambiguity is handled at KS4 (Ambiguity Explorer); no KS2-level tool for homophones, possessive apostrophes, or colon/semicolon/bullet punctuation.
title: Homophone Showdown
concept: A sentence with a missing word; class chooses there/their/they're and the picture/meaning updates instantly to confirm or correct.

strand: Shakespeare and the literary heritage (two plays, pre-1914 texts)
gapNote: Iambic Pentameter Scanner (KS4) supports Shakespearean verse rhythm but there is no KS3 tool for Shakespeare's language, staging or alternative interpretations.
title: Stage It Yourself
concept: Drag actors, lighting and blocking around a stage for a Shakespeare scene and watch how alternative staging changes the audience's interpretation.

strand: Creative and descriptive writing (AO5/AO6)
gapNote: Sentence Surgeon and Ambiguity Explorer build sentence craft but no app scaffolds a full narrative/descriptive piece with structure, tone and openings/endings.
title: Descriptive Zoom
concept: Pan and zoom into a picture stimulus; each zoom level prompts a sensory sentence, building a structured descriptive piece from wide shot to close detail.

strand: Literature: Shakespeare and the 19th-century novel (close analysis and context, AO1-AO3)
gapNote: Iambic Pentameter and Unreliable Narrator support method analysis but no app embeds set-text close reading with AO3 context (Macbeth, A Christmas Carol etc).
title: Context Layers
concept: Toggle context overlays (Jacobean kingship, Victorian poverty) onto a key extract and see how each layer changes a plausible AO3-informed reading.

strand: Literature: poetry analysis and comparison (anthology and unseen)
gapNote: Iambic Pentameter Scanner covers metre only; no app supports comparing anthology poems or analysing unseen poetry (Power and Conflict, Love and Relationships).
title: Poem Pair Comparer
concept: Choose two anthology poems; drag shared themes/methods into a Venn-style comparison and the app suggests comparative sentence stems.

strand: Close analysis of writers' craft: form, structure and language (AO2)
gapNote: KS4 tools (Sentence Surgeon, Iambic Pentameter) transfer somewhat but no KS5-level tool for sustained close reading at A-level depth across genres.
title: Close Reading Lab
concept: Layer annotations on a dense passage (lexis, syntax, form) and the app maps how micro-choices build a sustained AO2 argument.

strand: Contexts of production and reception (AO3)
gapNote: Literary Theory Lens touches interpretive frameworks but no app distinguishes contexts of production vs reception or evaluates contextual claims at A-level.
title: Production vs Reception
concept: Split-screen a text's moment of creation against later readings; drag context evidence to each side to show how meaning is re-made over time.

strand: Addition and subtraction (number bonds to 20, mental and written methods, inverse)
gapNote: No dedicated app builds/practises addition and subtraction fluency or number bonds within 20/100 at KS1; only word problems touch it.
title: Bonds Within 20 Workshop
concept: Drag counters across a part-whole model and double number line; the app shows the matching fact family and the inverse, so adding and subtracting facts appear together.

strand: Geometry: properties of shapes and position/direction (turns)
gapNote: EYFS shape sorter is pitched lower; no KS1 app for shape properties (sides, vertices, faces, symmetry) or turns/right angles.
title: Shape Detective
concept: Tap a 2D or 3D shape to count and highlight its sides, vertices, edges and faces, sort by property, and spot 2D faces on 3D solids.

strand: Statistics (pictograms, tally charts, block diagrams)
gapNote: Only a KS2 graph builder exists; nothing pitched at simple KS1 pictograms/tally/block diagrams with sorting.
title: Sort and Count Pictogram
concept: Drag objects into category columns to grow a live pictogram and tally chart, then answer totalling and comparing questions the app generates.

strand: Ratio and proportion, and early algebra (formulae, linear sequences, unknowns)
gapNote: KS3 sequences app partly serves linear sequences; nothing for KS2 ratio/proportion, scaling or simple formulae and missing-number algebra.
title: Recipe Scaler
concept: Drag a serving-size dial and watch ingredient quantities scale by ratio, with a double number line showing the multiplicative relationship.

strand: Algebra (notation, manipulation, expanding/factorising, equations and inequalities)
gapNote: Simultaneous equations are visualised, but expanding/factorising, collecting like terms, and solving linear equations/inequalities are not covered.
title: Algebra Tiles Workbench
concept: Drag algebra tiles to collect like terms, expand single brackets and factorise, with the symbolic expression updating as tiles move.

strand: Geometry and measures (area/volume, angles, Pythagoras, congruence/similarity, constructions)
gapNote: Transformations covered; area/volume of prisms, angle reasoning, Pythagoras and ruler-and-compass constructions need KS3-pitched apps.
title: Pythagoras Proof Squares
concept: Drag the squares on the sides of a right triangle and rearrange the area pieces to show a-squared + b-squared = c-squared dynamically.

strand: Algebra: equations, inequalities, simultaneous equations, rearranging
gapNote: Simultaneous (linear) covered; quadratic equations (factorising, formula, completing square), inequalities on graphs and rearranging formulae not covered.
title: Quadratic Solver Studio
concept: Enter a quadratic and watch factorising, completing the square and the formula solve it side by side, linked to where the curve crosses the x-axis.

strand: Probability (combined events, tree/Venn diagrams, conditional)
gapNote: Simulator covers experiments; tree diagrams, Venn diagrams and conditional probability at GCSE not explicitly built.
title: Tree Diagram Builder
concept: Construct branching tree diagrams where probabilities auto-multiply along branches and add across, including with/without replacement.

strand: Statistics (sampling, charts, averages, spread, histograms, cumulative frequency)
gapNote: Only a primary-pitched graph builder exists; GCSE histograms with unequal intervals, cumulative frequency and box plots are not covered.
title: Histogram and Frequency Density
concept: Drag class-interval widths and frequencies to see bars rescale by frequency density, contrasting with a naive bar chart.

strand: Pure: algebra, functions, proof, sequences and series
gapNote: Sequences/series touched (pitched lower); A-level functions (domain/range/composite/inverse, transformations, partial fractions, binomial expansion) and proof not covered.
title: Function Transformer
concept: Apply translations, stretches and reflections to f(x) with sliders, showing the algebraic change and domain/range and composite/inverse mapping diagrams.

strand: Pure: numerical methods and vectors
gapNote: 2D vectors covered (GCSE-level); numerical methods (change of sign, Newton-Raphson, trapezium rule) and A-level vector geometry not covered.
title: Newton-Raphson Visualiser
concept: Tap a starting point and watch tangents iterate towards a root, with change-of-sign intervals and the trapezium rule shown as an alternative.

strand: Statistics: sampling, data presentation, correlation and regression
gapNote: No A-level-pitched app for sampling methods, standard deviation, scatter/PMCC or regression lines; only primary graph builder exists.
title: Correlation and Regression Studio
concept: Drag points on a scatter plot to watch PMCC and the least-squares regression line update, with residuals and the large data set in view.

strand: Mechanics: kinematics, forces and Newton's laws, moments
gapNote: Kinematics (SUVAT) covered; forces and Newton's laws, connected particles/friction/inclined planes and moments not covered.
title: Force Diagram Lab
concept: Drag forces onto a body on a slope or pulley system, resolve them into components and apply F=ma to find acceleration and tension live.

strand: The natural world and living things (plants, animals, life processes)
gapNote: Plant Growth Diary (KS1) touches plants but there is no EYFS-pitched explore-living-things app for animals/minibeasts/observation
title: Minibeast Hunt
concept: Tap and reveal creatures in a garden scene, drag each to 'has legs / has wings / lives underground' baskets to sort living things by features

strand: Animals, including humans (groups, body parts, senses, basic needs)
gapNote: Senses Explorer covers the five senses but nothing covers animal groups (fish/birds/mammals), diet (herbivore/carnivore) or naming body parts at KS1
title: Animal Sorting Lab
concept: Drag animals into fish, amphibian, reptile, bird and mammal pens and toggle their diet to herbivore, carnivore or omnivore

strand: Living things and their habitats (Year 2: alive/dead/never-alive, microhabitats, food chains)
gapNote: Food Chain Builder (KS2) is pitched higher; no KS1 app on alive/dead/never-been-alive or matching animals to habitats
title: Alive, Dead or Never Alive?
concept: Sort objects (a tree, a rock, a fallen leaf, a toy) into three bins using the MRS GREN life processes as prompts

strand: Working scientifically (observing, questioning, simple tests, sorting)
gapNote: Sorting is embedded in content apps but there is no dedicated enquiry-skills app (asking questions, simple comparative tests)
title: Test It! Fair Test Maker
concept: Pick one thing to change and one to measure in a class-built simple test (e.g. which paper towel soaks up most), keeping everything else the same

strand: Plants (function of parts, water transport, reproduction)
gapNote: Plant Growth Diary is KS1; Photosynthesis app is KS4. No KS2 app on parts of a flower, pollination or water transport in stems
title: Inside a Flower
concept: Dissect a virtual flower, label stamen/carpel/petal and run a pollination-to-seed animation

strand: Living things and habitats (classification, environmental change, life cycles)
gapNote: Food Chain Builder covers feeding relationships well but not classification keys or life cycles (mammal/insect/amphibian/bird)
title: Build a Classification Key
concept: Drag yes/no questions to branch a set of organisms into a working dichotomous key the class tests live

strand: Forces and magnets (gravity, friction, air/water resistance, magnetism)
gapNote: Forces & Motion Simulator is KS3 (F=ma); KS2 needs simpler exploration of gravity, friction, levers and magnetic attraction/poles
title: Magnet Playground
concept: Drag magnets near materials to test attraction, flip poles to feel push/pull and find which materials are magnetic

strand: Electricity (simple circuits, switches, conductors)
gapNote: Circuit Builder is pitched at KS3; a simpler KS2 version (bulb lights / does not light, switches, conductors vs insulators) would fit the programme better
title: Light the Bulb
concept: Drag cells, wires, bulbs and a switch to complete a circuit, testing everyday objects as conductors or insulators

strand: Biology: material cycles and energy (photosynthesis, respiration, nutrition)
gapNote: Photosynthesis & Respiration app is pitched at KS4 (compensation point); a KS3-level version plus digestion/nutrition is missing
title: Leaf Factory (KS3)
concept: Adjust light and CO2 on a leaf and watch glucose and oxygen produced, with a simple word equation building live

strand: Biology: interactions, interdependence and health (ecosystems, gas exchange, reproduction, drugs/diet)
gapNote: Food chains touched only at KS2 level; no KS3 app on gas exchange, human reproduction, diet/drugs or interdependence
title: Ecosystem Balancer
concept: Adjust populations of producers, predators and prey and watch a pyramid of numbers stabilise or crash

strand: Biology: genetics and evolution (variation, inheritance, natural selection)
gapNote: Punnett Square app is KS4-level genetics; KS3 needs an accessible variation and natural-selection introduction
title: Variation Survey
concept: Collect class data on continuous and discontinuous traits and auto-plot histograms to show variation

strand: Chemistry: atoms, elements, compounds, mixtures and the periodic table
gapNote: Periodic Table Explorer is KS4; no KS3 app on elements vs compounds vs mixtures or separating techniques
title: Element, Compound or Mixture?
concept: Build substances from atom tiles and sort what you make, then run chromatography/distillation to separate mixtures

strand: Biology: cell biology, transport and microscopy
gapNote: Cells demo is pitched at KS3; no GCSE-level treatment of mitosis, stem cells, diffusion/osmosis/active transport or magnification calculations
title: Osmosis Visking Lab
concept: Set solute concentrations either side of a membrane and watch water move, plotting percentage mass change against concentration

strand: Biology: organisation, enzymes, transport systems and disease
gapNote: Enzyme Kinetics covers enzymes strongly; the wider strand (digestive/circulatory systems, communicable/non-communicable disease, immunity) is not covered
title: Heart and Blood Vessels
concept: Trace double circulation, label vessels and adjust heart rate and stroke volume to see cardiac output change

strand: Biology: ecology (ecosystems, cycles, biodiversity)
gapNote: Food chains only addressed at KS2; no GCSE app on quadrat/transect sampling, carbon/water cycles or human impact on biodiversity
title: Quadrat Sampler
concept: Throw quadrats across a virtual field, count species and calculate population estimates and distribution

strand: Chemistry: rates, equilibrium and organic chemistry
gapNote: Enzyme kinetics is biology; Organic Mechanisms is KS5. No GCSE app on rate of reaction (collision theory), Le Chatelier equilibrium or crude oil/fractional distillation and alkanes/alkenes
title: Rate of Reaction Lab
concept: Change temperature, concentration, surface area and catalyst and measure gas volume against time, linking to collision theory

strand: Physics: energy, electricity and the particle model
gapNote: Energy and circuits apps are pitched at KS3; GCSE needs power/efficiency calculations, I-V characteristics, mains electricity and specific heat/latent heat
title: I-V Characteristics
concept: Vary voltage across a resistor, filament lamp and diode and plot live I-V curves to compare components

strand: Physics: forces and motion (Newton's laws, momentum, stopping distance)
gapNote: Forces & Motion is KS3-level; GCSE needs distance-time/velocity-time graphs, momentum conservation and stopping/thinking/braking distances
title: Motion Graph Lab
concept: Drive a car and generate distance-time and velocity-time graphs live, reading off speed, acceleration and area under the curve

strand: Physics: magnetism, electromagnetism and atomic/nuclear physics
gapNote: Radioactive Decay covers half-life well; magnetism/electromagnetism (motor effect, induction, transformers) is not covered
title: Motor Effect Lab
concept: Place a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field, use Fleming's left-hand rule and watch a model motor spin

strand: Working scientifically and required practicals
gapNote: Several content apps double as practical demos but there is no app explicitly framed around the GCSE required practicals and their evaluation
title: Required Practical Trainer
concept: Run a virtual required practical, identify variables and errors, then answer exam-style method and analysis questions

strand: Biology: biological molecules, cells and exchange
gapNote: Transcription and enzyme kinetics are relevant but molecules (proteins/lipids/carbohydrates), membrane transport and gas/water exchange surfaces are not covered at A-level depth
title: Membrane Transport Explorer
concept: Model diffusion, facilitated diffusion, osmosis and active transport across a membrane with adjustable gradients and ATP

strand: Biology: genetics, evolution, biodiversity and ecosystems
gapNote: Punnett app supports monohybrid genetics; A-level dihybrid crosses, chi-squared, Hardy-Weinberg, gene pools and energy/nutrient cycling are missing
title: Hardy-Weinberg Simulator
concept: Set allele frequencies and selection pressures and watch a population evolve, calculating p and q each generation

strand: Chemistry: inorganic chemistry (periodicity, groups, transition metals)
gapNote: Periodic Table Explorer (KS4) gives trends; A-level Group 2/7, Period 3 oxides and transition-metal complexes/colours are not covered
title: Transition Metal Complexes
concept: Swap ligands around a central ion, predict shape and coordination number and watch the solution colour change

strand: Chemistry: organic chemistry and analysis (mechanisms, synthesis, spectroscopy)
gapNote: Organic Reaction Mechanisms covers mechanisms strongly; instrumental analysis (NMR, IR, mass spec) and multi-step synthesis routes are not covered
title: Spectra Detective
concept: Match IR, mass spec and proton/carbon NMR spectra to candidate molecules to deduce an unknown structure

strand: Physics: mechanics, materials and waves
gapNote: Wave interference and forces (KS3) are relevant but A-level projectiles, moments, momentum, materials (stress-strain/Young modulus) and SHM are not covered
title: Projectile and Moments Lab
concept: Launch projectiles and balance beams, resolving vectors and calculating range, moments and centre of mass live

strand: Physics: nuclear, particle physics and astrophysics/options
gapNote: Decay Simulator (KS4) gives half-life; A-level particle physics (standard model, quarks), nuclear energetics and the option topics (astrophysics etc.) are not covered
title: Particle Zoo
concept: Build hadrons from quarks, conserve charge/baryon number in interactions and read Feynman-style diagrams

strand: Lives and roles of people around us (community, jobs, then and now)
gapNote: Then-and-Now focuses on objects, not on people's roles/jobs in society which the Past and Present ELG also requires.
title: Who Helps Us, Then and Now
concept: Tap a community helper (nurse, postie, baker) to flip between an old photo and a modern one, with a read-aloud sentence comparing what their day looked like.

strand: Chronology and time vocabulary (sequencing, past/present/before/after)
gapNote: Old/New Sort gives a two-way past/now split but no ordered timeline or graded time vocabulary (long ago, recently, decade).
title: When Did It Happen? Class Timeline
concept: Whole-class drag of photos (grandparents' school, Victorian street, today) onto a left-to-right time ribbon; the app snaps each and reads the matching time phrase aloud.

strand: Disciplinary knowledge: using simple sources and how we find out about the past
gapNote: Sorting old/new objects touches artefacts but does not ask 'how do we know?' using photos, diaries or buildings as evidence.
title: History Detective: Clues from the Past
concept: An old object, a photo and a (simplified) diary line appear; children tap each source and answer 'what does this tell us?', building a class wall of evidence about one event.

strand: A non-European society (early Islamic Baghdad, Maya, or Benin c.AD 900)
gapNote: The comparator may include non-European civilisations but there is no dedicated Baghdad/Maya/Benin depth study; pitched as comparison not depth.
title: House of Wisdom: Baghdad c.900
concept: Walk a 3D-ish circular city; pupils enter the library, observatory and bazaar, each unlocking an interactive on Islamic advances in maths, astronomy and medicine and how knowledge travelled.

strand: Chronological understanding: securing the big picture across periods
gapNote: Timeline Builder is pitched KS3 and abstract; KS2 needs a scaled period-by-period 'big picture' ribbon (BC/AD, overlapping civilisations).
title: The Big Picture Timeline
concept: A proportionally scaled BC/AD ribbon where pupils place studied periods (Egypt, Romans, Vikings) and see at a glance which overlapped and how long ago each was.

strand: Second-order concepts: cause, change, similarity/difference, significance
gapNote: Comparison covers similarity/difference but causation and significance for KS2 are not addressed at this phase (existing causation apps are KS3/KS4).
title: Why Did It Happen? Cause Cards
concept: Pupils drag picture-cause cards (e.g. why the Romans invaded) into 'big reason / small reason' rings and rank them, with a class vote revealing the spread of opinion.

strand: Britain, Europe and the wider world 1901-present, including the mandatory Holocaust study
gapNote: Propaganda decoder (tagged KS4) touches 20th-century material but there is no Holocaust study or WWI/WWII/welfare-state content at KS3.
title: Voices of the Holocaust: Why It Matters
concept: A respectful, age-appropriate timeline of persecution to liberation, where pupils unlock survivor testimony and reflect on choices, resistance and remembrance (no graphic imagery).

strand: British depth study with historic environment (Norman/Elizabethan/Restoration England + linked site)
gapNote: Source tools support the skills but no app delivers the substantive depth content or the historic-environment site enquiry.
title: Elizabethan England Investigator
concept: Explore court, religion and the Armada through interactive panels, then 'visit' a linked historic site and reason about what its physical features reveal.

strand: Non-British depth study (conflict/crisis, e.g. Cold War, Russia/USSR, USA Civil Rights)
gapNote: Revolution-vs-Rebellion touches conflict comparison but does not teach a specified non-British depth period in detail.
title: Cold War Flashpoints
concept: A world map of Berlin, Cuba and Vietnam; students step through each crisis, choosing superpower responses and watching a tension meter to grasp escalation and detente.

strand: Breadth study: change and continuity over c.100+ years (Component 1)
gapNote: Change/Continuity Analyser (tagged KS3) is conceptually relevant but not pitched to A-level long-arc breadth analysis or a specified breadth topic.
title: Breadth Study Change Mapper
concept: Students plot political, economic, social and religious threads across a 100-year breadth topic on parallel lanes, then test periodisation by sliding turning-point markers and justifying them.

strand: Depth study: causal complexity and contingency over 25-50 years (Component 2)
gapNote: Causation tools (KS3/KS4-tagged) help but there is no A-level depth tool handling contingency, counterfactual reasoning or layered causation.
title: Contingency and Counterfactual Lab
concept: Students remove or alter a single factor in a depth-study crisis and the app reasons out how the chain of events might have changed, surfacing the difference between underlying and trigger causes.

strand: Primary source evaluation in context (AO2: provenance, value, utility)
gapNote: GCSE/KS3-pitched source tools exist but nothing models A-level provenance interrogation (NOP) and purpose-dependent value judgement at the required rigour.
title: Source Value NOP Workbench
concept: Students dissect a primary source by Nature, Origin and Purpose, then watch how its assessed value shifts as the enquiry question changes, building substantiated AO2 judgements.

strand: Exploring the natural world (plants, animals, contrasting environments)
gapNote: Weather is touched but plants, animals and contrasting habitats are not interactively explored at EYFS level.
title: Habitat Explorer Garden
concept: Drag animals and plants into the right environment (pond, woodland, hot place, cold place) and watch them thrive or not, with read-aloud labels.

strand: Human and physical geography: weather patterns, hot/cold areas, basic vocabulary
gapNote: Seaside app teaches human/physical features but hot/cold areas relative to Equator and Poles is not addressed.
title: Equator Hot and Cold
concept: Spin a globe with the Equator and Poles lit up; drag a thermometer to any spot to reveal hot, temperate or cold and matching scenery.

strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork: maps, compass directions, aerial photos, fieldwork
gapNote: Fieldwork tallying is covered; simple N/S/E/W compass work and aerial-to-plan recognition pitched at KS1 is missing (map-skills app is KS2 level).
title: Bird's Eye School
concept: Flip between a ground photo and the aerial view of a school, then build a simple plan map using picture symbols and a key.

strand: Place knowledge: comparative region study (UK, Europe, Americas)
gapNote: Biomes explorer gives regional environment data but not a structured side-by-side region comparison.
title: Region Compare Lab
concept: Pick a UK region and an overseas region; sliders reveal climate, land use and population side by side for guided comparison.

strand: Physical geography: rivers, mountains, volcanoes, earthquakes and the water cycle
gapNote: Strong KS3-pitched apps exist for water cycle, rivers and tectonics but none simplified to KS2 level; mountains as landforms not covered.
title: Volcano Cross-Section
concept: Click layers of a volcano to see magma rise and erupt, then label the parts in a drag-drop diagram at primary reading level.

strand: Ecosystems, biomes and human impacts (deforestation, desertification)
gapNote: Climate graphs touch biome classification but ecosystem structure and human impacts are not interactive.
title: Rainforest Under Pressure
concept: Adjust logging, farming and protection sliders on a rainforest and watch biodiversity, carbon and indigenous land respond over time.

strand: Human geography: population, migration and urbanisation
gapNote: Population and urban structure apps exist but are tagged KS4; migration flows not covered at KS3.
title: Migration Flows Map
concept: Drag push and pull factors onto source and destination countries and watch animated migration arrows and impacts update.

strand: Human geography: economic activity, development and globalisation
gapNote: Development indicators app exists but is KS4-pitched; globalisation and economic sectors not covered.
title: Globalisation Web
concept: Click a transnational company and watch its supply chain, factories and markets light up across a world map with trade-off cards.

strand: The living world: ecosystems, rainforests and cold/desert environments
gapNote: Biome climate covered but rainforest deforestation and cold/desert environment management not modelled at GCSE depth.
title: Ecosystem Nutrient Cycle
concept: Drag biomass, litter and soil stores in a rainforest nutrient cycle and see deforestation break the loop.

strand: Geographical skills and fieldwork enquiry (cartographic, graphical, statistical)
gapNote: Graph building and scatter plots present, but OS map skills, statistical techniques and the fieldwork enquiry cycle are not directly taught.
title: Fieldwork Enquiry Builder
concept: Walk through a coastal or urban enquiry: choose sampling, plot collected data and evaluate reliability with guided prompts.

strand: Optional physical: glaciers, hot deserts, ecosystems under stress or hazards
gapNote: Glaciation and hazards touched but not at A-level systems depth (e.g. hazard risk equation, glacial mass balance).
title: Glacier Mass Balance
concept: Adjust accumulation and ablation over years to model glacier advance/retreat and equilibrium line altitude shifts.

strand: Optional human: contemporary urban, population/environment or resource security
gapNote: Urban models and population structure exist but are GCSE-pitched; resource security and population-environment relationships not modelled at A-level.
title: Population and Resources Modeller
concept: Run Malthus vs Boserup scenarios by adjusting population, technology and resources and watch carrying-capacity outcomes diverge.

strand: Geographical and quantitative skills and the independent investigation (NEA)
gapNote: Some data visualisation present but A-level statistical tests (Spearman, chi-square, significance) and the enquiry process are not covered.
title: Stats Test Chooser
concept: Feed in a sample dataset and the tool walks through selecting and running Spearman's rank or chi-square, interpreting significance live.

strand: Special stories and special people from religious traditions
gapNote: Only a KS1 stories app exists; nothing pitched at Reception with picture-book story exploration.
title: Story Carpet
concept: Tap-through animated picture-book stories from each tradition with simple 'what happened next' sequencing for the youngest children.

strand: Belonging: families, communities and respect for diversity
gapNote: Belonging is only touched at KS1; no EYFS-level 'we all belong' activity celebrating diversity.
title: All Are Welcome
concept: Build a class picture by dragging diverse families, foods and celebrations into a shared community scene that lights up as it fills.

strand: Festivals and special times (Christmas, Easter, Eid, Diwali, Hanukkah)
gapNote: Only an EYFS celebrations app exists; nothing KS1-specific linking festivals to their stories and meanings.
title: Festival Calendar Wheel
concept: Spin a year wheel to each festival, then match the story, food and symbol that belong to it across Christianity, Islam, Judaism and Hinduism.

strand: Special places of worship and what makes them special
gapNote: Covered at EYFS only; a KS1 app would go deeper into named features and their uses.
title: Inside the Place of Worship
concept: Pan around 360-degree church, mosque, mandir and synagogue interiors, tapping hotspots that name each feature and explain its purpose.

strand: Beliefs about God, creation and the human condition across traditions
gapNote: Creation app is pitched at KS3; no KS2-level app comparing core beliefs about God and creation.
title: What Do They Believe About God?
concept: Slide between traditions to see child-friendly statements about God (one, many forms, no creator) with linked stories and symbols.

strand: Sacred texts: structure, authority and interpretation
gapNote: Sacred Texts Comparator is a KS3 analysis tool; KS2 needs a simpler 'how is this book used and treated' explorer.
title: Handle With Reverence
concept: Explore how each holy book (Bible, Qur'an, Torah, Guru Granth Sahib) is stored, carried and read, dragging the right care-practice to each text.

strand: Festivals, pilgrimage and the religious year
gapNote: Pilgrimage Explorer is KS3-pitched; no KS2 app linking festivals and pilgrimage to their meanings for believers.
title: Journey and Festival Map
concept: Click pilgrimage destinations and festival markers on a world map to unlock why each matters and what believers do there.

strand: Diversity within traditions (denominations, schools, Sunni/Shia, lived religion)
gapNote: Venn compares whole worldviews but does not surface internal diversity (e.g. denominations, madhhabs, Orthodox/Reform).
title: One Religion, Many Branches
concept: Expand a branching tree for each religion (Catholic/Orthodox/Protestant, Sunni/Shia/Sufi) to see where beliefs and practices diverge and why.

strand: Non-religious worldviews: Humanism, atheism, agnosticism, secular ethics
gapNote: Worldview Venn can include non-religious views but there is no dedicated app teaching Humanism/atheism/agnosticism in their own terms.
title: The Non-Religious Map
concept: Position yourself on sliders (belief in God, source of morality, life after death) and see where Humanist, atheist and agnostic positions sit.

strand: Ethical reasoning: applying religious and non-religious frameworks to issues
gapNote: Ethics Dilemma is pitched at KS4; KS3 needs an introductory framework-comparison tool on contemporary issues.
title: Big Questions Debate Builder
concept: Pick an issue (environment, animal rights), pull in religious and Humanist viewpoints, and assemble a balanced two-sided argument on screen.

strand: Study of two religions: beliefs, teachings and practices (GCSE Component 1)
gapNote: KS3 comparator apps give breadth but nothing supports the GCSE in-depth, exam-keyed study of two chosen religions including common/divergent views.
title: GCSE Beliefs Revision Engine
concept: Select your two GCSE religions and drill into beliefs, teachings and practices with quote-evidence cards and common-vs-divergent view toggles.

strand: Thematic studies: relationships, religion and life, peace and conflict, crime and punishment, human rights (GCSE Component 2)
gapNote: Rights/responsibilities and general dilemmas touch some themes; no coverage of relationships/families, peace and conflict, or crime and punishment with religious teachings.
title: Theme Explorer: Peace and Conflict
concept: Work a conflict scenario through just war, pacifism and forgiveness teachings, dragging scripture and non-religious views onto each position.

strand: Existence of God and revelation (arguments and their critiques)
gapNote: Argument Mapper builds structures but is not anchored to the design/First Cause/revelation arguments and counter-arguments at GCSE level.
title: Does God Exist? Argument Lab
concept: Construct the design and First Cause arguments brick by brick, then fire counter-arguments at them and judge whether they still stand.

strand: Evaluative writing and constructing reasoned arguments (AO2 / 12-mark technique)
gapNote: Argument Mapper helps structure logic but does not model GCSE-style evaluative essay writing against the mark scheme.
title: 12-Mark Essay Builder
concept: Drag argument, counter-argument, scripture and judgement blocks into an evaluative paragraph that scores live against the GCSE AO2 descriptors.

strand: Normative ethical theories (natural law, situation ethics, virtue ethics, Kant, utilitarianism)
gapNote: Medical Ethics and trolley apps apply frameworks but there is no dedicated tool teaching and contrasting the normative theories themselves at A-level depth.
title: Ethical Theory Workbench
concept: Feed one dilemma to natural law, situation ethics, virtue ethics, Kant and Bentham in parallel and compare their reasoning and verdicts side by side.

strand: Curiosity about other cultures and celebrating linguistic diversity
gapNote: Hello World shows flags but there is no app exploring festivals, food or daily life of other cultures at EYFS level.
title: Around the World Story Carpet
concept: A projected world map where tapping a country plays a short greeting, a flag, and a culture snapshot (a festival, a food, an animal) so children explore diversity by pointing and listening together.

strand: Speaking and repeating familiar words with accurate pronunciation
gapNote: Vocabulary apps present words but neither focuses on modelling and rehearsing pronunciation (say-after-me, record-and-compare) at KS1 level.
title: Say It Like a Native
concept: Children tap a picture, hear a native speaker, then echo it; a friendly sound-meter shows how closely the rhythm matched, encouraging whole-class call-and-response.

strand: Listening: understand spoken language and respond
gapNote: Classroom Phrases involves some listening but there is no dedicated KS2 listening-comprehension app for gist and detail in short passages.
title: Listen and Show
concept: Pupils hear a short spoken description (a person, a room, the weather) and drag items onto a scene to show understanding, with replay and an embedded transcript reveal.

strand: Speaking: conversations, asking and answering questions, presenting orally
gapNote: Weather Reporter supports a presentation task and Classroom Phrases gives stock phrases, but two-way conversation/question-answer practice is thin.
title: Two-Way Talk Cards
concept: A flip-card dialogue mat where the teacher and class take turns: tap a question card, a model answer appears, then pupils substitute their own details to rebuild the exchange.

strand: Phonics: link spelling, sound and meaning of words
gapNote: Pronunciation Rules Guide covers sound-spelling but is pitched at KS3; no KS2-friendly phonics introduction.
title: Sound Spotter
concept: A projected grid of target-language graphemes; tap one to hear it and see three example words, then a class game where pupils sort picture-words by their hidden sound.

strand: Writing: write phrases from memory and adapt them; describe people, places and things
gapNote: Weather Reporter uses sentence building for one topic; broader adapt-a-phrase writing across topics is not covered at KS2.
title: Phrase Remix
concept: Pupils start from a model sentence and swap colour-coded slots (noun, adjective, place) to write new descriptions, building a class gallery of self-made sentences.

strand: Basic grammar: gender, articles and high-frequency verb conjugation
gapNote: Gender Patterns and Sentence Builder exist but are pitched at KS3; no gentle KS2 introduction to gender and simple verbs.
title: Le, La, Les Sorting Game
concept: Drag nouns into the correct article basket with instant feedback and a hint that reveals the ending pattern, scaling difficulty as the class succeeds.

strand: Listening: obtain information from spoken language and transcribe
gapNote: Sound Discrimination Trainer is KS4-pitched and tests phoneme contrasts, not gist/detail comprehension of passages at KS3.
title: Catch the Detail
concept: Authentic-style audio clips with a live transcription challenge: pupils type what they hear word by word, with adjustable speed and a reveal that highlights what they missed.

strand: Writing: connected prose and creative expression
gapNote: Sentence Builder helps at sentence level but there is no KS3 tool for building paragraphs/connected prose or creative writing.
title: Paragraph Climber
concept: Pupils assemble a short paragraph by linking sentences with connectives and opinion phrases, earning a complexity score that rewards subordination and tense variety.

strand: Vocabulary breadth across GCSE themes and false-friend awareness
gapNote: False Friends and the word planner help, but there is no app systematically drilling the ~1,200-1,500 word GCSE vocabulary lists by theme with spaced retrieval.
title: Theme Vocab Sprint
concept: A spaced-retrieval flashcard arena organised by the three GCSE themes; the class races a leaderboard while the algorithm resurfaces words pupils keep missing.

strand: Reading and bidirectional translation (TL to English and English to TL)
gapNote: Translation into and out of the target language is mandatory at GCSE but no app practises it; False Friends only touches one pitfall.
title: Translation Trainer
concept: Sentences to translate both directions with a chunk-by-chunk marking scheme that flags lost tense, agreement or word-order points and scores like an examiner.

strand: Advanced grammar and sophisticated language manipulation
gapNote: Existing grammar apps are KS4-pitched; there is no A-level tool for complex structures such as subjunctive, conditional perfect or advanced syntax.
title: Subjunctive Studio
concept: Trigger-phrase prompts where pupils decide if the subjunctive is required and conjugate it, with a live explanation panel justifying each mood choice.

strand: Listening and bidirectional translation at A-level standard
gapNote: Sound discrimination is KS4 level; no app handles A-level listening passages or the demanding two-way translation in Paper 1.
title: Advanced Translation Lab
concept: Idiomatic A-level passages to render both directions, with a side panel surfacing register, idiom and structural pitfalls and an examiner-style mark scheme.

strand: Cultural, social and political themes of target-language societies
gapNote: Literary analysis touches theme, but the four A-level societal/political themes (immigration, politics, cyber-society, occupation/resistance) are not addressed as content.
title: Theme Dossier Builder
concept: An interactive knowledge organiser for each A-level theme where pupils tag authentic articles and clips with arguments and statistics to build an essay-ready evidence bank.

strand: Movement, gesture and physical expression
gapNote: Emotion masks touch facial expression but no app explores whole-body movement, mime or gesture for the youngest children.
title: Move Like a...
concept: Tap an animal or character card and the on-screen figure animates the movement, then children copy and the class votes which body shape matches best.

strand: Voice, expression and speaking aloud
gapNote: Puppet theatre lets children speak in role but no app explicitly develops vocal expression, volume, pace or clarity.
title: Voice Dial
concept: A dial sets loud/soft, fast/slow, happy/sad; the class reads the same line and listens back, matching the voice to the meaning.

strand: Characterisation and emotions in role
gapNote: Freeze frames and puppets imply character but no app focuses on building a character's feelings and motives at KS1 level.
title: Character Builder
concept: Drag a feeling, a want and a problem onto a character card, then the whole class acts the resulting mini-scene and discusses what changed.

strand: Performing scripts and poems aloud
gapNote: Script Writer produces text but no app supports rehearsing and performing a script aloud with expression and cues.
title: Read-Aloud Rehearsal Room
concept: Highlights each character's lines in turn with pace and pause markers; the class performs a script extract while the tool keeps the cueing.

strand: Characterisation, status and role
gapNote: The spinner randomises characters but no app deliberately develops character, status or hot-seating.
title: Hot Seat Generator
concept: Pick a character; the screen serves probing questions for hot-seating while peers vote whether the answer stays in role.

strand: Drama techniques and conventions (thought-tracking, narration, mime)
gapNote: Improv scenarios are covered but the named conventions (thought-tracking, narration, mime, conscience alley) are not taught explicitly.
title: Convention Cards
concept: Flip a card to reveal a technique with a worked example animation, then the class applies it to a shared stimulus on screen.

strand: Performing from a script and text interpretation
gapNote: Blocking covers movement but no KS3 app focuses on interpreting and rehearsing a set script with subtext.
title: Subtext Decoder
concept: A scripted line shows on screen; students drag possible subtexts and intentions, then see how delivery changes the meaning.

strand: Evaluating and responding to live/recorded theatre
gapNote: Mastery Studio is pitched at KS5; KS3 lacks an age-appropriate tool for analysing a watched performance.
title: Watch and Critique
concept: Pause-point prompts overlay a clip asking about acting, design and impact; responses collate into a class review.

strand: Devising drama (and devising log)
gapNote: Devising toolkit is KS3; no GCSE-specific tool supports the assessed devising process and the written devising log.
title: Devising Log Builder
concept: Guides students through stimulus, intentions, rehearsal and evaluation entries against AO1/AO2/AO4, exporting a structured log draft.

strand: Study of a set text from a performance perspective
gapNote: Mastery Studio analyses scripts but is pitched at KS5/A-level; no GCSE-level set-text-as-performer tool with the section B exam focus.
title: Set Text Director
concept: For a key extract, students assign staging, character intention and design choices to each beat, then generate model exam answers in the page-to-stage style.

strand: Reviewing live theatre
gapNote: Live Theatre Review Builder exists but is labelled KS5; a GCSE-pitched version with the GCSE mark scheme is missing.
title: GCSE Live Review Coach
concept: Scaffolds a review around acting, design and audience impact with GCSE band descriptors and exemplar phrases.

strand: Devising original theatre influenced by a practitioner
gapNote: Mastery Studio covers conventions and application but no A-level tool drives the assessed devising-in-the-style-of-a-practitioner process and working notebook.
title: Devising in Style Studio
concept: Pick a practitioner; the tool constrains rehearsal choices to that style and scaffolds the working-notebook reflective entries.

strand: Scripted performance and interpretation (monologue/group)
gapNote: Monologue Coach is GCSE-pitched; no A-level-level interpretation tool for extended scripted performance and concept development.
title: Interpretation Concept Board
concept: Build a directorial concept for a scene with annotated staging, design and intention layers, then test it against the text's themes.

strand: Rights, responsibilities and the rights of the child
gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html is an RE/ethics scenario balancer, not a primary citizenship tool on the UN rights of the child.
title: Rights of the Child Explorer
concept: An interactive carousel of UNCRC rights where pupils match each right to a real classroom scenario and balance it against a matching responsibility.

strand: Rights, liberties, responsibilities and the rule of law
gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html touches rights vs responsibilities but is an RE scenario tool, not a KS3 citizenship treatment of liberties and the rule of law.
title: Balance of Liberties
concept: Students adjust sliders (free speech vs harm, privacy vs security) on real case studies and see the trade-offs and legal limits update live.

strand: Human rights, equality and the law (Equality Act, ECHR)
gapNote: re-rights-responsibilities.html is an RE/ethics balancer; nothing covers the Equality Act, protected characteristics or the ECHR at KS4.
title: Equality Act in Action
concept: Students judge real-style discrimination scenarios against the nine protected characteristics and see whether the law was breached and what redress applies.

strand: Self-regulation and managing feelings
gapNote: Emotion Thermometer and Zones of Regulation exist but are pitched at older readers and assume independent reading; nothing EYFS-pitched with pictures/colours.
title: Feelings Faces Match
concept: Big animated faces show emotions; children drag a matching colour or calming action (deep breath, ask for help) onto each face, teacher-projected with audio prompts.

strand: Self-confidence, independence and trying new things
gapNote: Growth Mindset Reframer touches perseverance but uses written statements unsuitable for EYFS.
title: The Try-Again Tree
concept: Each time the class taps to have a go at a tricky task the tree grows a leaf; models that effort and mistakes help us grow, fully visual.

strand: Caring friendships and getting on/falling out
gapNote: Friendship Network Mapper and Conflict Resolution Flowchart exist but read older; nothing simple on what makes a good friend at KS1.
title: Good Friend or Not?
concept: Animated short scenes; class signals thumbs up/down and the app reveals why an action is kind or unkind, building a shared friendship charter.

strand: Recognising and naming feelings
gapNote: Emotion Thermometer covers intensity but is text-heavy; KS1 needs a vocabulary-building tool.
title: Feelings Word Wall
concept: Tap a face to grow a word bank of feelings vocabulary; class matches a feeling to a 'what could help' card, projected and discussed.

strand: Respectful relationships and treating others fairly
gapNote: Perspective Swap Simulator builds empathy but does not explicitly cover respect, courtesy and stereotypes; no dedicated app.
title: Respect Detectives
concept: Class examines classroom and online scenes for respectful vs disrespectful behaviour, builds a respect rule, and tests it against tricky edge cases.

strand: Internet safety, screen time and being critical online
gapNote: Scenario Navigator touches cyberbullying but nothing on screen-time balance, reliability of online content, or sharing/privacy.
title: Real or Fake Feed
concept: A mock social feed the class swipes through, judging whether posts are true, an advert, or unsafe to share, with a running 'critical thinking' score.

strand: Healthy and respectful relationships, consent and boundaries
gapNote: Restorative Conversation and Perspective Swap support relationship repair but nothing addresses consent, boundaries or healthy intimate relationships.
title: Boundaries and Consent Lab
concept: Branching peer scenarios where the class decides what consent looks like, spots pressure and coercion, and practises assertive responses.

strand: Online life: digital wellbeing, harms, pornography and media literacy
gapNote: Scenario Navigator covers bullying but nothing on online harms, misinformation, body image or pornography myths at KS3.
title: Decode the Feed
concept: Class dissects influencer/advert/edited-image posts, exposing how media shapes body image and beliefs, scoring their media-literacy calls.

strand: Bullying, prejudice, discrimination and being an upstander
gapNote: Scenario Navigator handles bullying but not prejudice/discrimination or active upstander strategies.
title: Upstander Simulator
concept: Bystander scenarios with prejudice and discrimination; class chooses how to safely intervene and sees the ripple effect on the group.

strand: Recognising abuse, coercive control, harassment and seeking help
gapNote: Scenario Navigator and Restorative Conversation touch conflict but not abuse, coercive control, harassment or safeguarding routes.
title: Red Flags and Routes to Help
concept: Class spots warning signs of unhealthy/abusive relationships across scenarios and maps the safest route to support and reporting.

strand: Mental health, stress, self-care and exam wellbeing
gapNote: Zones of Regulation and self-regulation tools help but nothing is pitched at KS4 stress, study pressure or help-seeking.
title: Stress and Resilience Studio
concept: Class builds a personalised coping toolkit against realistic exam and life stressors and tests it in a simulated pressure week.

strand: Online safety, digital footprint and media in adulthood
gapNote: Scenario Navigator covers bullying only; nothing on digital footprint, online reputation, sharing images or scams at KS4.
title: Your Digital Footprint
concept: Class audits a fictional teen's posts as a future employer/college might, then reverses to clean and protect the footprint.

strand: Mental health, independence and life transitions
gapNote: Self-regulation tools transfer but nothing addresses the move to independence, university/work stress or where adults get help.
title: Transition Resilience Lab
concept: Simulated first weeks of independent life (money, isolation, workload) where the class builds and stress-tests coping and help-seeking plans.

strand: Using clear models, exemplars and success criteria
gapNote: Scaffolding planner touches on supports but does not help build annotated WAGOLL exemplars or success criteria.
title: Exemplar and Success Criteria Builder
concept: Annotate a 'what a good one looks like' model against co-created success criteria pupils can self-assess against.

strand: Deliberate and guided practice
gapNote: Scaffolding planner addresses support but not the design of independent practice tasks at the right difficulty.
title: Practice Task Designer
concept: Build sets of practice items that move from guided to independent, calibrating difficulty and minimal effective help.

strand: Efficient and sustainable marking practice
gapNote: Feedback builder helps phrase feedback but does not address whole-class feedback or workload-efficient marking models.
title: Whole-Class Feedback Sheet Builder
concept: Turn marking observations into a single whole-class feedback sheet with common errors, model answers and next steps.

strand: Supporting pupils with SEND
gapNote: Differentiation planner is generic; nothing translates EHCP or SEND-register needs into specific classroom adaptations.
title: SEND Classroom Adaptation Planner
concept: Convert a pupil's identified needs into concrete, low-workload teaching adaptations using the graduated approach.

strand: Lesson and unit planning
gapNote: Lesson timer addresses pacing only; there is no structured lesson or unit planning template grounded in the framework.
title: Evidence-Informed Lesson Planner
concept: Build a lesson around recap, instruction, practice and check, prompting for cognitive-science-aligned choices at each phase.

strand: Attainment 8 composition and the three buckets (English, maths, EBacc, open)
gapNote: P8 calculator implies A8 slots but no dedicated bucket-weighting and 10-slot fill explorer.
title: Attainment 8 Bucket Builder
concept: Drag grades into the English (double), maths (double), EBacc and open buckets and watch A8 and the 10-slot fill recalculate live.

strand: Prior-attainment banding (low, middle, high) of measures
gapNote: Value Added uses prior attainment as the x-axis but there is no tool to split headline measures by KS2 prior-attainment band.
title: Prior-Attainment Band Splitter
concept: Break P8, A8 and Basics by low, middle and high KS2 prior-attainment bands to expose where progress is strongest.

strand: Intersectional disadvantage (multiple overlapping characteristics)
gapNote: Demographic breakdown shows single characteristics but not the compounding effect of overlapping ones.
title: Intersectional Gap Explorer
concept: Filter by combined characteristics (e.g. disadvantaged SEND boys) to surface compound gaps masked by single-factor views.

strand: Closing-the-gap trajectory over time
gapNote: Gap visualiser is largely point-in-time; no multi-year narrowing/widening trend view.
title: Gap Trajectory Trendline
concept: Chart the disadvantage gap across three to five years with projection of when parity would be reached at current rate.

strand: Persistent and severe absence identification
gapNote: Patterns app shows attendance but no explicit PA (under 90%) and SA (under 50%) cohort flagging against thresholds.
title: Persistent Absence Early-Warning Board
concept: Flag pupils crossing the 90% and 50% thresholds with sessions-needed-to-recover and RAG escalation.

strand: On-track vs off-track aggregation and forecasting
gapNote: Flight Path Tracker shows individual trajectories but no whole-cohort forecast of projected headline measures.
title: Cohort Outcome Forecaster
concept: Aggregate current working-at grades into a projected P8/A8 with optimistic and cautious scenario bands.

strand: Comparable outcomes and statistical equating awareness
gapNote: Boundary explorer shows mark thresholds but not the comparable-outcomes principle that drives year-on-year shifts.
title: Comparable Outcomes Explainer
concept: Interactive model of how prior-attainment-anchored standard-setting moves boundaries even when papers vary in difficulty.

strand: Grade-point conversion and scaling (9-1 to points)
gapNote: Conversion is implicit in P8 and boundary tools but no standalone scale reference covering vocational point equivalents.
title: Grade-to-Points Converter
concept: Convert 9-1, legacy A*-G and approved vocational grades to DfE point scores for mixed-qualification cohorts.

strand: Effect size and statistical significance of interventions
gapNote: Tracker logs interventions but does not compute effect size or whether change exceeds noise.
title: Intervention Effect-Size Calculator
concept: Compute Cohen's d and confidence intervals on pre/post data to test whether an intervention genuinely worked.
